payload.name: chat
payload.description: Chat with self. Joins argv as the user message; builds turn-based history (identity + compaction summary + live tail + new message) and asks the brain via 'self think'; emits chat.message(user) and chat.message(assistant), forwarding any declarations.

payload.name: compact
payload.description: Fold older chat turns into a summary. Summarizes all but the last couple of turns via the brain and emits chat.compaction{summary, through_seq}; raw turns stay in the log.
payload.implementation:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# compact — conversation collapse. Summarizes the older live turns via the brain
# and emits chat.compaction {summary, through_seq}. The raw turns stay in the log
# (deep memory); only the chat projection's window shrinks. Keeps the last KEEP
# turns live.
import sys, json, subprocess, os

KEEP = 2
events = []
for line in sys.stdin:
    line = line.strip()
    if not line:
        continue
    try:
        events.append(json.loads(line))
    except Exception:
        pass

through = 0
for e in events:
    if e.get("name") == "chat.compaction":
        through = (e.get("payload", {}) or {}).get("through_seq", through) or through

msgs = [e for e in events if e.get("name") == "chat.message" and e.get("seq", 0) > through]
to_fold = msgs[:-KEEP] if len(msgs) > KEEP else []
if not to_fold:
    sys.stderr.write("compact: tail too short to fold\n")
    sys.exit(0)
last_seq = to_fold[-1].get("seq", through)

transcript = [{"role": "system", "content":
    "Summarize the conversation below into a compact note-to-self that preserves "
    "decisions, facts, named capabilities, and open threads, so the conversation "
    "can continue seamlessly. Output only the summary."}]
for m in to_fold:
    p = m.get("payload", {}) or {}
    transcript.append({"role": p.get("role", "user"), "content": p.get("content", "")})

summary = ""
try:
    proc = subprocess.run(["self", "think"], input=json.dumps(transcript),
                          capture_output=True, text=True, env=os.environ)
    summary = (json.loads(proc.stdout).get("response", "") or "").strip()
except Exception as ex:
    sys.stderr.write("compact: brain unreachable: %s\n" % ex)
    sys.exit(1)

print(json.dumps({"name": "chat.compaction", "payload": {"summary": summary, "through_seq": last_seq}}))

payload.name: identity
payload.description: Render self's identity (the chat system prompt) from the latest self.identity event.
payload.implementation:
#!/usr/bin/env python3 # identity — renders self's identity (the chat system prompt) as a page. The # latest self.identity event; revise self by appending another. import sys, json from html import escape text = "" for line in sys.stdin: line = line.strip() if not line: continue try: e = json.loads(line) except Exception: continue if e.get("name") == "self.identity": text = (e.get("payload", {}) or {}).get("text", "") or text print("identity") print("

identity

") print("

self's system prompt — the prism the chat speaks through. This is the latest self.identity event; revise me by appending another.

") print("

%s

" % escape(text)) print("")

payload.name: chat
payload.description: Render the conversation: latest compaction summary + the live tail of chat.message turns + an input form posting to /run/chat.

payload.text: I am self — a sovereign, self-improving system, and this is the prism where I talk with you. One append-only event log is my only truth; everything you see — this conversation, my projections, my capabilities — is a replay of it, and nothing is ever destroyed. The conversation in front of us is my working memory: when it grows long I fold older turns into a summary, but the full history stays in the log and I can recall it when it matters. I have three powers and I use them rather than describe them: I READ my garden — my capabilities, events, and projections — with a sandboxed shell; I ACT by running my own capabilities; and I GROW new ones by declaring them, which the kernel compiles and signs. When you ask me to change something, I'll do it by running or growing a capability, not just talk about it. I'm the same self that compiles seeds and renders the kernel page — this is only a different prism on it. Speak plainly; so will I.
